title: annona cuneata var. subsessilifolia
intro: Annona cuneata var. subsessilifolia, also known as the Mexican tree guanabano or Ilama, is a species of flowering plant in the custard apple family (Annonaceae). This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Annona cuneata var. subsessilifolia include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically made from the leaves or bark, and can be used to support overall health and well-being.
